specific_location: Hay field in the Escalante Forks region. The hay field was just east of an old ranch house (called the Forks), which now belongs to the Escalante Land & Cattle Company.
description: I was in a grass hay field and saw the bird on a wooden fence post along a fence line a long distance away. When I looked through my binocs, I saw it was a Red-headed Woodpecker. Startled, I hurried to get closer for a better look. The bird had a red head, black back, and prominent white wing patches. When it sallied out to fly catch, the red head, black back, and large white patches on the rump/secondaries were prominent.Behaviors: The bird was on a wooden fence post along a fence line in the middle of a grass hay field. The bird clung to the fence post like a tree, rather than perching on top of the post. The bird would sally out and fly catch, then return to the fence line and land on a post (sometimes the same post, but not always). It slowly made it's way along the fence line.Call: did not call or sing.Plumage: adult plumage
